title: Severe Bleeding Control
immediate_action: Apply direct pressure immediately. Call emergency services.

steps.instruction: Apply FIRM direct pressure to the wound using a clean cloth, gauze, or even clothing. Press HARD.
steps.tip: Use gloves if available. If nothing else, use your bare hand — saving life is priority.

steps.instruction: Do NOT remove the cloth if it soaks through. Add more cloth on top and keep pressing.
steps.tip: Removing soaked material disrupts clotting. Layer more material on top.

steps.instruction: If bleeding is from a limb, elevate the injured limb above the heart while maintaining pressure.
steps.tip: Gravity helps reduce blood flow to the wound.

steps.instruction: If bleeding doesn't stop with direct pressure, apply a tourniquet 2-3 inches above the wound (limbs only). Tighten until bleeding stops. Note the time.
steps.tip: A belt, tie, or torn cloth can serve as improvised tourniquet. Write the time on the person's skin.

steps.instruction: Keep the person warm and lying down. Monitor for signs of shock: pale skin, rapid breathing, confusion.
steps.tip: Cover with blankets. Do NOT elevate legs if spinal/head injury is suspected.

do_not: Do NOT remove objects embedded in the wound
Do NOT apply tourniquet to neck, head, or torso
Do NOT give the person anything to drink
